nativeRange.habitat: Rubble and low patch reefs in areas of current, typically on outer reef slopes
nativeRange.biotope: Tropical reef rubble and patch-reef zones reported at roughly 2–40 m depth

diet.notes: In the wild has been documented feeding on zooplankton in the water column. In aquaria generally accepts vitamin-enriched frozen mysis and brine shrimp, finely chopped meaty foods, and quality marine flake or pellets. Several small feedings per day tend to suit the species' active mid-water foraging style.

avoidWith: other male fairy wrasses in the same tank

summary: A small, brightly colored fairy wrasse from the Philippines and Indonesia, generally considered a peaceful and reef-safe addition to mature medium-to-large reef aquariums.
careNotes: Cirrhilabrus rubripinnis inhabits outer-reef rubble and low patch reefs in areas of current across the Western Central Pacific (Philippines and Indonesia), reported from roughly 2–40 m depth. Sources disagree modestly on adult size — LiveAquaria advertises about 4 in (~10.2 cm), FishBase records 9.1 cm TL, and AquaticCommunity cites about 3.1 in (~8 cm); most aquarium specimens arrive at 8–10 cm. Tank-size recommendations also diverge: AquaticCommunity suggests ~208 L (55 gal), LiveAquaria recommends ~340 L (90 gal), and some hobby guides recommend ~400 L (100 gal). We have leaned toward the larger end of the range given the species' active mid-water swimming style and the value of a long footprint with open swimming lanes; 300 L is offered as a sensible minimum. Temperature also shows a spread between LiveAquaria's 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C) recommendation and FishBase's wild preferred range of 28–29.1°C; the 22–27°C range used here brackets typical reef-tank conditions. Generally peaceful and considered reef-safe — coral and ornamental-invert nipping is not commonly reported — though AquaticCommunity notes individuals can become more assertive over time. Like most Cirrhilabrus, this species is a notable jumper and has been reported to bolt straight upward when startled; a tightly fitted lid or eurobrace is strongly recommended. Best kept as a single male, or as a male with one or more females added simultaneously to form a small harem; multiple males in the same tank tend to fight. Individuals are protogynous (female-first sequential hermaphrodites), and males display heightened color intensity with notably enlarged red dorsal and anal fins during nuptial displays.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Species page used for water parameters, tank size (90 gal / ~340 L), diet, temperament (peaceful), care level (easy), reef-safe rating, and jumping note.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirmed family Labridae, order Eupercaria, max length 9.1 cm TL, depth range 2–40 m, distribution Philippines/Indonesia, and zooplankton-feeding behavior in small male-led groups.

additional.name: AquaticCommunity
additional.notes: Cross-checked sizing (3.1 in / ~8 cm) and tank size (55 gal / ~208 L). Noted as 'semi aggressive — may become quite aggressive as time goes on,' which biases overall temperament slightly above LiveAquaria's 'peaceful.'

additional.name: ReefApp Encyclopedia
additional.notes: Cross-checked tank-size recommendation (100 gal / ~380 L minimum, 200 gal preferred); confirms the cautious-larger lean in the tank.minVolumeLiters figure.
